supply_chain.dependencies: No single supplier is material, although for reasons of quality assurance, regulatory requirements, cost effectiveness, availability or uniqueness of design, certain materials components may be sourced from a single supplier or a limited number of suppliers
risks: Supply chains could be disrupted by supplier capacity constraints, bankruptcy or exiting of the business
Decreased availability or increased cost of key raw materials or commodities

External events such as global economic downturns and macroeconomic trends, sanctions and trade restrictions, natural disasters, pandemic health issues, geopolitical developments, war, terrorist actions
Reliance upon sole or limited sources of supply for certain materials or components could cause production interruptions, delays and inefficiencies

regulatory_risks: FDA regulation of the drug discovery and development process
Increased FDA regulation of laboratory-developed tests
DEA regulations
OSHA regulations
EPA regulations
State boards of pharmacy regulations
State health department regulations
EMA regulations
EU member states regulations
Environmental laws and regulations (Toxic Substances Control Act, Resource Conservation and Recovery Act, Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Safe Drinking Water Act, CERCLA)
Changes in governmental regulations may reduce demand for products or increase expenses
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (IRA) drug price negotiation provisions
Government contract compliance regulations
Export control and economic sanctions laws
Import laws and regulations
Tariffs and trade protection measures
Data privacy and data protection laws (EU GDPR, state-level US regulations, China data localization laws)
Environmental, social and governance (ESG) legal and regulatory requirements
Income tax laws and regulations changes
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rate of 15%

sanction_risks: International sanctions in response to certain state actions
Tariffs imposed by the U.S. on goods from other countries and tariffs imposed by other countries on U.S. goods, including tariffs on various imports from China and by China on certain U.S. goods
Export control and economic sanctions laws affecting transactions with certain customers
